assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.
The identified foreign exchange exposure arises from an intragroup balance or transaction.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposures to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk, including highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
